title: CVE-2026-5027: Unpatched Langflow Flaw
summary: [P1] CVE-2026-5027 is a high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, which has come under active exploitation. This vulnerability allows for unauthenticated RCE. Why now: Reported exploitation in the wild (confidence: 0.90)
classification_reasoning: This vulnerability allows for unauthenticated RCE, which is a classic example of an RCE problem type, and it affects the framework layer as it is related to the Langflow platform.

primary.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE

enrichment.lede: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE.

title: CVE-2026-11417: OS Command Injection in aws-cdk-lib
summary: [P1] CVE-2026-11417 is an OS command injection issue in the NodejsFunction local bundling pipeline in aws-cdk-lib, which may allow an actor to execute arbitrary commands. This vulnerability is not yet patched and has not been exploited in the wild. Why now: Reported attribution (unverified): none (confidence: 0.80)
classification_reasoning: This vulnerability allows for arbitrary code execution, which is a classic example of an RCE problem type, and it affects the framework layer as it is related to the aws-cdk-lib NodejsFunction.

primary.title: CVE-2026-11417 - OS Command Injection in aws-cdk-lib NodejsFunction bundling
